data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic, a Series B-stage developer in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $600M+ in cumulative fundraising. The addressable peer set in this narrow frontier-tech subsector comprises four entities: two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private competitors (Hermeus, Venus Aerospace), though direct stage-matched comparables remain sparse.

Given the scarcity of same-stage peers in Supersonic & Hypersonic, peer-median valuation was not applicable; a median across the fragmented comp set would lack statistical reliability. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression analysis, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier—typical for Series B advancement to the next funding stage—to the $1.5B last post-money valuation. This method yields an implied forward valuation of $2.9B.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $2.4B to $3.3B, with $2.9B as the median estimate. The wide band reflects two material uncertainties: (1) the absence of sufficient same-stage peer data limits cross-validation, and (2) the nascent and heterogeneous nature of hypersonic development creates significant stage-step variability across the cohort. The $2.9B midpoint represents a 93% increase over current valuation, consistent with typical Series B-to-C expansion dynamics. Investors should weight this estimate heavily against non-financial factors: regulatory pathway clarity, technical de-risking, and customer commitment validation.
